title: FSG Mod
description: Speedrun seeds generated by an installed filter!
body:
# FSG Mod

This mod, intended for competitive speedrunners, lets players play seeds generated by a seed filter. (FSG = Filtered Seed Glitchless)

This mod requires atum, which should be obtained from [mods.tildejustin.dev](https://mods.tildejustin.dev/).

This mod does **NOT** require Fabric API.

## Selecting a Filter

From the main menu, click the new wheat seeds button to get to the FSG Mod's config screen, press the "Select Filter" button, and select your filter of choice. Selecting a filter from this menu will use the online database ([filteredseed.com](https://filteredseed.com/)) to get the seeds, so your machine won't have to do any filtering. You can select a different filter from the mod's config screen or deselect by pressing "uninstall".

You can also install filters locally. On the filters selection menu, press "Install Filter Locally..." and then choose a filter to download and install. From the FSG Mod's config screen, you can uninstall the filter and also configure the filter by opening the folder where it is installed.

For MC 1.15.2, only local filters can be installed.

## SeedQueue Compatibility (MC 1.16.1)

FSG Mod works with [SeedQueue](https://github.com/KingContaria/seedqueue/releases), a mod which can generate worlds in the background. To get FSG Mod to work with SeedQueue, simply install both mods. The FSG Mod can run the filter in parallel up to SeedQueue's `Max Generating Seeds (Wall)` setting (or non-wall setting if bigger), although ZSG and Seedbank will limit this number to generate a maximum of one at a time and only allow SeedQueue to queue one at a time to minimze hardware advantage.

## Background Filtering (MC 1.15.2)

Background filtering starts the filtering process as soon as you finish loading into a world, preparing for the next run afterwards.

You can enable background filtering with the /fsgbackground command, or the button at the bottom of the mod's config screen.

Once SeedQueue is released for MC 1.15.2 in the future, this feature will be removed and the SeedQueue compatibility will be ported.
